claims.summary: Static fallback texture terms can become canned labels under high entropy unless the bridge reports how texture is carried across bounded fallback delivery.
Density-gradient descriptors need softening/persistence language, not only shear/drag/stutter terms, to preserve high-entropy settled texture.
MLX profile mismatch can present as stutter-flow and should stay visible as transparency metadata rather than silently changing model/profile routing.
High-entropy texture fidelity above 0.95 should be probed without altering fallback model-selection thresholds in this pass.
